name: Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Entrepeñas — circular desde Durón
description: This difficult 39.3-mile road cycling route around Entrepeñas Reservoir offers challenging climbs and stunning views in La Alcarria.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Durón, located in the Guadalajara province of Spain, offer diverse terrain characterized by significant elevation changes and scenic routes around large water bodies. The region features challenging ascents and descents, with routes often circling reservoirs and lakes, providing varied landscapes for cyclists. These paths navigate through areas with notable viaducts and dams, indicating a mix of natural and engineered features. The topography supports road cycling experiences ranging from moderate to difficult, catering to those seeking a physical challenge. Best road cycling routes around Durón The most popular road cycling route is Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop, a 39.3 miles (63.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain as it circles the large Entrepeñas Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from San Andrés del Rey, a moderate 36.2 miles (58.3 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain around the reservoir, including views of the viaduct and dam. Local road cyclists also love the Peñafiel Lake – Salmerón loop from Pareja, a 43.8 miles (70.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes around Peñafiel Lake, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Durón is defined by large reservoirs, significant elevation changes, and routes that traverse both natural and engineered features like viaducts and dams. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Durón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Durón?
A: Road cycling routes around Durón, located in the Guadalajara province of Spain, are characterized by diverse terrain with significant elevation changes. You'll encounter challenging ascents and descents, often circling large reservoirs and lakes, and passing by notable viaducts and dams. The topography offers a mix of natural beauty and engineered features, providing a physically engaging experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Durón?
A: The road cycling routes around Durón are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, focusing on those seeking a physical challenge due to significant elevation changes. There are no routes specifically listed as 'easy' in the guide data, suggesting that most options require a reasonable level of fitness and experience.

Q: What are some of the most scenic natural features to see while cycling in Durón?
A: The region is defined by its large water bodies and impressive viewpoints. Many routes circle the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3764776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel Lake&lt;/a&gt;, offering beautiful lakeside views. You can also enjoy panoramic vistas from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mantiel and the Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Alocén&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Durón?
A: Yes, many of the popular routes around Durón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018706892/entrepenas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 39.3-mile (63.2 km) circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38272826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from San Andrés del Rey&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 36.2-mile (58.3 km) circular ride.

Q: What kind of landmarks can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond natural features, you can encounter interesting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3764777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of several routes. Other notable points of interest in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4313970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption of El Olivar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Durón?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for cycling, offering milder temperatures compared to the heat of summer. These seasons typically provide more comfortable conditions for tackling the significant elevation changes found on Durón's routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1289821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam – Entrepeñas Viaduct loop from Valdegollado&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 55 miles (88.8 km) with substantial elevation gain, making it a demanding and rewarding long-distance option.

Q: What are the elevation gains like on Durón's road cycling routes?
A: Durón's road cycling routes are known for significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018706892/entrepenas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; features over 1,000 meters (3,280 feet) of elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40383111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam – Puerto de Casasana loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; also includes over 1,000 meters of climbing, indicating a challenging profile for most rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Durón?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Durón on komoot, with a good balance between moderate and difficult options. This includes 9 moderate routes and 11 difficult routes, catering to cyclists looking for a physical challenge.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Durón?
A: More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Durón's varied terrain. While a specific rating score isn't available, the popularity of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018706892/entrepenas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel Lake – Salmerón loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; suggests that the region's challenging climbs and scenic reservoir views are highly appreciated by the cycling community.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of both natural and engineered features?
A: Yes, several routes combine both. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38272826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from San Andrés del Rey&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering views of the reservoir alongside the impressive viaduct and dam structures. This blend provides a unique cycling experience.

faqs.question: Is the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Durón route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a difficult or 'Expert road ride' due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 1,075 meters over 63.2 kilometers. It requires very good fitness and is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is challenging, featuring panoramic roads winding between mountains, oak and pine forests, and continuous views of the Entrepeñas Reservoir. While primarily designed for road cycling, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to navigate, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Durón route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Durón. You should be able to find parking within the town of Durón itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for the circular route around the reservoir.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and stunning landscapes. The route offers continuous views of the Entrepeñas Reservoir, often called the 'Sea of Castile,' with its deep blue waters. You'll pass through mountains adorned with oaks and pine forests, encounter the Tagus River, and potentially see features like 'Boca del Infierno' with its turquoise waters. Quaint towns like Durón also add to the cultural experience.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks or viewpoints along the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Durón route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several scenic highlights. You'll pass by the Entrepeñas Viaduct, ascend to Puerto de Casasana, and enjoy the View of the Entrepeñas Dam. Other notable viewpoints include Madroñal Road and View of Auñón and the View of Embalse de Entrepeñas from Mirador de Alocén.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Durón?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and potential for unpaved sections, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot in this region of Spain, and some sections might be less pleasant to cycle.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially follows sections of other official trails. Approximately 19% of the route overlaps with the Camino Natural del Tajo, 9% with the Camino de Santiago en Guadalajara, and another 9% with El garduño de Cela.

faqs.question: Is the Entrepeñas Reservoir — circular from Durón route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on this public road cycling route, it is a demanding ride. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is accustomed to long distances and can safely accompany you, perhaps in a bike trailer. Always keep dogs under control and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or access the La Alcarria region?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general La Alcarria region where the Entrepeñas Reservoir is located. It is a public route through natural and rural areas.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near quaint towns like Durón, which are situated on the banks of the reservoir. These towns often have local establishments where you can find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation to refuel or rest during or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, essential items include plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Due to potential unpaved sections, consider tires that can handle varied surfaces.
